As we do that, that should be a helpful framework for us to think about, like, okay, these are the type of questions that are going to highly engage the personas you care about.
How do we create experiences and content that are amazing answers to that?

And then as we think about that, as we research what's out there, we see what the competition is doing, we talk to, through the sprint phase, things that we commonly do are talk to your technical teams.
If, you know, customers are open to it, like recent strategy sprints we've kicked off, we did in the first week, I think it was three or four customer interviews.
It's just to really understand them at a deeper level.
And we can synthesize all these different things together to basically say, okay, we have some hypotheses of what can be really strong experiences to create.
Let's start to put those tests out into market, see if they get traction.
Similar to the VAP case, that was kind of like us.
We saw a big opportunity, but we need to test to see if to validate that before we went too heavy.
And we're at a phase now where we feel like we're starting to see validation.
So then we're accelerating, essentially.
so that's what that means for each customer.
It tends to be a bit different.
And that's why we start with the strategy for it, essentially, is to understand where should we go.
Moving fast is not our challenge.
We want to make sure we move fast on the right stuff.
For the BAP thing, you mentioned that this is one of the things that now you can do hundreds of these, like the same format, like different use cases.
You're going to create pages for each one of them.
Exactly, yeah.
So if I recall, it's like 3,000 plus use cases they identify.
We started with a core set of 100.
We posted those live.
Within days, we were starting to see them get indexed, which is really promising to see that happen fast.
And then just high engagement of those pages.
so that's where it was, okay, that doesn't mean we'll put out a batch of 3,000 tomorrow.
Usually, you want to chunk these things out and be mindful.
You don't want to create a bad signal for Google to think you're doing anything too shady.
And this is in no way shady.
It's just like sometimes if you launch 5,000 pages in a day, that could raise some red flags for Google.
So maybe we go to 200 the following week.
Okay, let's monitor that 200.
Is that following the same trajectory of ranking and engagement?
Okay, yeah, no, we feel good.
Let's continue to ramp up.
So we're the users of Slack and Notion.
And we meet weekly with our accounts.
between the weekly meetings, between the async comms and all that, like we can start to dial in and essentially like plan all those.
us.
right.

This is definitely, like, very interesting and also, well, I think the only thing is it's, it's, it feels a bit, like, risky, just like, invest 20K and, like, a 12-month commitment, but, um, I think for me, to build more conviction, it's just, like, trying to learn or get some insights on what can be achieved with broccoli, like, a high-level overview.

This happy case was super helpful, um, but what are some things that broccoli can execute, because, uh, I think for us, it's, the question is always going to be, like, do we get a person in-house, uh, because I think context is the most important thing here, like, really understanding our customers, or is it worth working with GrowthX and our team can just focus on more other, like, more, experiments, but, uh,
Uh, yeah, I think, yeah, this is helpful.
So, uh, is there any other company that's live, uh, and I can just see the work that you've done for them?
Uh, yeah, of course.
I mean, uh, let me try to think of another one that I can pull up.
Um, uh, you know, uh, so, like, another example, and I can show you a little bit of, like, how just to, we've worked with, um, um, just give me a second.
I'm pull out some links.
Uh, we work with this company called Galileo, uh, one of our oldest customers.
Um, they are, uh, in the AI, uh, eval space.
Um, we've done, uh, a variety of different things with them.
And so, um, you know, as mentioned, like, you know, we're big users of Slack.
And so just to give you a view of, like, what that kind of looks like, like, this is, you know, essentially like all the teams teams in.
You know, we're giving different updates on kind of like what we're working on, posting, you know, weekly goals, et cetera.
And then, you know, from like some examples of like content we've done for them, we've done a ton of content for them in the past, you know, handful of months.
And I don't know if you saw that chart, but seeing a lot of great growth for them recently.
Another example of like where we can do things that are kind of interesting, the reasons it came to mind is you and Bruno were talking about video.
Earlier, I hadn't had a chance to actually watch your video, but I'll go back.
Certainly now intrigued too, but I love video because of just the different ways that can be repurposed, right?
So like you may be doing the webinar, you may be doing something you want to share in social, whatever it may be, right?
And, you know, that can then go on YouTube, that can go on LinkedIn, you check in some of those boxes in terms of like coverage across varying channels and engaging people.
But what we can also do is...
Just that video content into our workflows and create content off of that.
And then that can also further than the distribution of value of that video.
And so in this case, Galileo does a weekly podcast.
We created a workflow and this workflow can be like highly adapted to different use cases.
Right.
But like for them, what we wanted to take this was this isn't just like taking the transcript and just like creating headers and beautifying it.
And what the intention of this workflow really is to understand the story and subject of the conversation and build a rich article around that.
We even can do different things around this.
I know there's some in here, but like it can pull out quotes and like attribute those quotes to who said it.
You know, there's just a variety of different like fun things you can do to make sure this is like actually like a comprehensive and useful piece of content.
And what I love about this is like then, you know, they're already doing this.

We can create an article that has some SEO value off the back of it, but also, like, whether it's this podcast or let's say it's an on-demand webinar, this also then can be content that you can leverage as part of your email nurture streams, right?

Like, you know, this type of, like, instead of a common tactic would be, like, taking an on-demand webinar and, like, putting it on, like, a success page and then, like, putting it into your email nurture stream.

that's fine, but, like, if you did something like that and you had an article that sits alongside that and someone's on their phone and they can read it, they can always watch the video if they want or whatever.
It's just, like, that multi-medium approach can be really effective to engaging different type of people because people like to learn in different ways, right?
And so you have this video and now you have it on YouTube, you have it on LinkedIn, you have it on your site, you now have an article with it.

And so it's things like that that we're always going to try to see if we can find, like, compound effects of what we do rather than just, like, yes, we can find, like, a great set of topics, coordinate with you on LinkedIn.

Those topics are, we build a thorough content OS and Airtable that coordinates on all this, but also can do a lot of enhancements and things that we can layer on the edges that, you know, to me is like a bit of like a differentiator in how we would work.

Yeah, another idea, Kyle, would be the podcast, right, AJ, where we could actually take those and transform the transcripts and kind of leverage that towards creating more content for you as you already have the podcast embedded in a page.

know, typically what we do from here, AJ, is, you know, it seems like, you know, there's some interest, still a little bit of like, hey, you know, how can I be confident in the ROI?

We can do then Thursday.
I think that would be better for me.
Okay.

Do you have a preferred time?
I'm not sure where you're at time-wise.
PT.
Okay.
We can do 9 a.m.
On Thursday the 26th.
Yeah.
Yeah.
That works.
That works.
Yeah.
Perfect.
Bruno, can you set up a 45-minute at that time?
Yeah, man.
I'll send that out right now.
And then do you use LAC?
Yeah.
Cool.
I'll set up a LAC channel as well.
Feed us as much as you can.
You know, like competitors and kind of sites that you're using as inspiration.
Already working with Mappy, think that's a great success story for you to look at, right?

What else, Kyle?
What other contacts would you like to have before the next meeting?
No, that's helpful.
I mean, somewhat related.
If there's any brands that you model or think after, like, I love what they do from a growth perspective, that's always helpful as well.

Sure.
From a design and just, like, through a brand point of view, we really like Postalk.
Oh, yeah, okay, yep.
So our new website is also going to be, like, that fun vibe, which is non-obvious.

So that is something.

to meet you, AJ.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory call between Broccoli and GrowthX to explore potential collaboration on growth and content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Broccoli is a fast-growing startup (0 to $2M revenue in 12 months) looking to accelerate growth through marketing and content](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [GrowthX offers AI-powered content creation and growth strategy, starting with a $20K 6-8 week strategy sprint](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=868.0)
  - [Follow-up meeting scheduled to discuss specific opportunities for Broccoli and dive deeper into GrowthX's approach](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=976.0)

## Topics

### Broccoli's Current Situation and Goals

  - [Founded 4 years ago, pivoted to current product 12 months ago](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Grown from $0 to $2M revenue and team of 3 to 9 people in 12 months](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=373.0)
  - [Recently raised capital, looking to invest in growth](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=383.0)
  - [Current growth primarily through inbound leads and trade shows](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=424.0)
  - [Seeking to establish marketing foundation and accelerate growth](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=547.0)

### GrowthX's Offering and Approach

  - [Two-phase engagement: 6-8 week strategy sprint ($20K) followed by ongoing execution](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=76.0)
  - [Focus on editorial content creation powered by AI workflows and human expertise](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)
  - [Team includes former TechCrunch editor-in-chief and experienced growth leaders](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0)
  - [Emphasis on developing unique, high-quality content experiences beyond just blog posts](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=1744.0)
  - [Examples shared: interactive use case pages for Vappy, podcast content repurposing for Galileo](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=1649.0)

### Potential Collaboration Areas

  - [Developing content strategy aligned with Broccoli's target personas and use cases](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=1923.0)
  - [Creating unique content experiences to differentiate from competitors](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=1744.0)
  - [Leveraging existing assets (e.g. video content) for multi-channel distribution](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=2253.0)
  - [Exploring programmatic content opportunities (e.g. industry-specific landing pages)](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=1703.0)

### Next Steps Discussion

  - [AJ expressed interest but some hesitation about committing to long-term engagement](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=2102.0)
  - [GrowthX team to prepare high-level overview of potential opportunities for Broccoli](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=2437.0)
  - [Follow-up meeting scheduled for Thursday, June 26th at 9am PT](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=2548.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Bruno to send calendar invite and set up Slack channel for collaboration](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=2561.0)
  - [AJ to share additional context: competitor info, inspirational brands/sites](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=2573.0)
  - [GrowthX team to prepare specific ideas and opportunities for Broccoli](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=2652.0)
  - [Follow-up meeting to dive deeper into GrowthX's approach and potential ROI](https://fathom.video/share/qqHxrT_ioqw5RcTQGF_DiYyXcEZ1ksLs?tab=summary&timestamp=2460.0)

action_items.description: Prep for follow-up: Broccoli-specific opportunities, strategy artifacts, platform overview
Schedule 45-min follow-up meeting for Thu 26th 9am PT; create Slack channel for AJ
Send to GrowthX via Slack: competitor info, inspiration sites, Broccoli context
